commands.review_summary:
The PR adds an internal Telegram message-cache `latestBeforeMatching` helper and uses it to find `/new` or hard `/reset` session boundaries without calling `recentBefore(..., Number.MAX_SAFE_INTEGER)`.

PR surface: Source +39. Total +39 across 1 file.

Reproducibility: yes. by source inspection: current main calls `recentBefore` with `Number.MAX_SAFE_INTEGER` before filtering session-boundary commands. I did not run a live or large-cache performance repro in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Contributor action is needed for real after-fix proof, and a maintainer may choose whether source/unit evidence is enough for this draft internal perf change.

target.title: [codex] Avoid broad Telegram reset boundary scan
target.body:
## Summary

- Add a targeted Telegram message-cache helper for finding the latest matching message before the current message.
- Use it for `/new` and hard `/reset` session-boundary detection instead of materializing every previous cached message with `recentBefore(..., Number.MAX_SAFE_INTEGER)`.
- Leave the existing `recentBefore` and `around` behavior unchanged.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/telegram/src/message-cache.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.extensions.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/extensions.tsbuildinfo`
- `git diff --check`
target.author: monkeyleet
